item.title: Australia's hard road to gun control
item.description:
In April 1996 a lone 28-year-old gunman entered the historic Port Arthur tourist site (a former penal colony) in Tasmania and shot dead 20 innocent people in the first 90 seconds. That gunman (whom I refuse to name) went on to murder a total of 35 people, seriously wounding 18 others. Reports later revealed he was able to purchase his semi-automatic assault weapon from a gun dealer without holding the required gun licence. Overnight Prime Minister Howard took action. He knew such an atrocity must never happen again in our country.
Howard made the brave and – let’s be clear - unpopular move to take on the gun lobby. He introduced a government gun buy back scheme which saw the government buy back (and destruction) of more than 700,000 Australian owned guns. And he essentially forced the states to sign an agreement for nationwide gun law reforms. “The National Firearms Programme Implementation Act 1996, restricts the private ownership of semi-automatic rifles, semi-automatic shotguns and pump-action shotguns as well as introducing uniform firearms licensing.” We have not had a single mass shooting since that very day. We are a safer country because of that call. Our children can go to school, students can wander around their university campuses, young people can gather at music festivals, our families can go to church or to the mall or the cinema knowing that the chances an armed assailant is going to turn up is all but impossible.
Australia isn’t perfect. We all know that. We have much to do in truly valuing and respecting our Indigenous communities. We have incredible work to do in the area of male violence against women and children. We struggle to show the compassion needed towards those in detention. We have a serious issue with alcohol. I could go on. But by God we got something right and it’s this. So thank you, Prime Minister Howard. In 1996 you made a tough call that changed our nation and we’re all safer because you did. Feature Image: YouTube / ABC's 7:30 TLDR: after the port Arthur massacre, Australia's prime minister carries through legislation that restricts sale of high caliber, automatic and semi automatic weapons. And it worked. Front page edit. While this is Rebecca's story, I was on the Tasman peninsular that day and the sound of the police and ambulance sirens going past is something I'll always remember. No civilian needs an AR-15.
Rebecca Sparrow wrote this article in 2018
Unfortunately this will get down voted
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=j2G0j3FWKuE
item.dc:creator: TravisTravel

item.title: Don’t be fooled. They know EXACTLY what they’re doing.
item.dc:creator: MoBullieMom

item.title: Need a good book to read? Well... I did a thing! Wrote a 267 page, 12 chapter memoir about my 37 years of existence. Extremely therapeutic for me.
item.description: My book is called: "Elizabeth" My Journey Through Control. And is available on Amazon for paperback and ebook. The back of my book reads: "Do you like sex, drugs, and rock n' roll? Do you know what jails, institutions, and death are all about? I don't want to be controlled, but can I control myself? Who says I have to live by society's rules?" This book is a raw and close look into grief, mental illness, chronic homelessness, drug addiction, and institutionalization methods used to "help". I just published in December, and am getting a lot of positive feedback on my story (300 sold/downloaded). It's priced low to sell as i'd rather have the readers than jack up prices for income. That's why I sent copies to the libraries to lend as well. I just ended a 5 day free ebook promo, but I'll run one again soon! Thanks for giving it a try. I really think you'll like it. I know, I know that's what you'd expect me to say... But for real, it's pretty awesome. Comes with a 35 song soundtrack to listen to and read if you please. If you're interested here's a link to Amazon. You can read a free sample there and see if it's your type of book : https://www.amazon.com/gp/aw/d/B09MZXWQY1/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0?ie=UTF8&qid=&sr=
item.dc:creator: zyrany

item.dc:creator: Aspen2000

item.title: "Borrowed" Memes to Help Your Day
item.description:
A few have sound- this is one.
Do you still have your sound on? You might want to. Or you might not- that's fine.
You might turn your sound back on if you turned it off.
Okay, this really does require sound. If you are hard of hearing, here's what's going on: The man on the bench starts singing Bon Jovi's "Living on a Prayer" acapella. Gradually, the other park visitors join in until most of the park is singing with him. This is not a pre-arranged flash mob of gifted singers but just everyday people coming together in song. Try to avoid doom scrolling too much today. Remember to get some fresh air when you can. If you can't find the helpers, try to be one yourself, even if it is just in tiny ways.
item.dc:creator: ThatOneFriendWhoTriesTooHard

item.dc:creator: derploma

item.title: Hard day...One less brother left.
item.description: He kept going back and forth between casual chat and unhinged rants of hatred. Slagged any democrat run state, complained that homeless people could sleep in tents in California instead of being forced out by police, railed against abortion rights. Acidic toward trans people, and paranoidly obsessed with pedophiles. He said he would murder a pedophile in cold blood without hesitation, even if it was me, or our parents. I told him murdering people would make him somewhat worse than a pedophile and he lost his mind, said I wasn't allowed near his children, I was defending pedophiles, I must be one, etc. Then went right back to chatting. When we stopped, I confronted him and demanded an apology. He apologized, hesitantly abd, in my opinion, insincerely. Told him "Someone who would say that to me is not my brother", and he got mad, doubled down, told me to fuck off. Got in a shouting match in front of his 3 year old daughter and both our parents. I told him in front of everyone that I don't consider him part of my family anymore. Feeling pretty jumbled about it all, but I am sick of helping him when he's just a poisonous, unstable asshole. I'm so sick of tolerating him. Just want to scream into the void with a shitty meme. Thank you for reading, if you still are. All men are my brothers, all women are my sisters, everyone between is my beloved sibling. I hope you all have an amazing day, week, year and life. Front Page Edit: Thank you all for the concern, the advice, and the support. I want to reply to every comment but there are so many. I love you all, and I want the best for you all.
item.dc:creator: LordOfTheFattys

item.title: Almost one month of no alcohol.
item.description: It’s like I have a new face. Obviously I have on makeup in the second pic but no filter (stupid I have to even say this lol) I’ve drank every day for the last 4-5 years. My relationship with alcohol is changing and I couldn’t be happier. Edit: christ on a cracker, here’s no makeup for you mfs: https://imgur.com/a/pwz4kZF Thanks everyone for the kind and supportive words :)

item.title: Crazy like this needs to be behind bars.
item.description: I don't know if this is a repost, I tried quickly scrolling through new and usersub but didn't see this. I really only want to bring this to the attention of a larger audience. This person needs to be reported to the authorities, it has to be taken seriously. I also tried finding out if he was already arrested, but no luck there either. Here's a link to the story. https://www.lgbtqnation.com/2022/05/right-wing-extremist-promises-hunt-lgbtq-allies-pride-month-shocking-video/
